commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0f48eb4ca9450c0335a19c878388f67e01bb613f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `skipDeferral` restart-intent flag, threads it through the scheduled SIGUSR1 restart path, and makes the gateway close-stage reply drain use a zero timeout for skip-deferral restarts.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +44. Total +55 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence remaining path: start a normal safe restart that enters active deferral, then issue a skip-deferral restart while the deferral poll is active; the bypass branch emits without the new intent, so close-stage reply drain still receives the normal budget.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof, and the branch needs a small code/test repair before it can be treated as merge-ready.

target.title: fix(gateway): skip pending reply drain on skip-deferral restart
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw gateway restart --safe --skip-deferral` skips the pre-restart active-work deferral check but still waits on the close-stage pending reply drain, because the `skipDeferral` flag is not threaded through the restart intent to the SIGUSR1 handler and server close logic.

The restart scheduler in `restart.ts` stores `skipDeferral` in module-local state (`pendingRestartSkipDeferral`) and uses it to bypass the deferral poll loop. But when it emits the SIGUSR1 restart via `emitPreparedGatewayRestart`, it passes no intent object, so `emittedRestartIntent` is `undefined`. The SIGUSR1 handler in `run-loop.ts` then gets a `restartIntent` without `skipDeferral`, and `resolveRestartCloseDrainTimeoutMs()` computes the full drain budget. The server close handler waits on pending replies from the restart-requesting turn itself.

The fix threads `skipDeferral` through the restart intent:

1. Adds `skipDeferral?: boolean` to `GatewayRestartIntent` and `RestartIntentOptions`
2. When `scheduledSkipDeferral` is true, passes `{ skipDeferral: true }` as the intent to `emitPreparedGatewayRestart`
3. In `resolveRestartCloseDrainTimeoutMs()`, returns `0` when `restartIntent?.skipDeferral` is true

Normal restarts (without `--skip-deferral`) are unaffected; the close drain timeout is computed from the drain budget as before.

Fixes #89528

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `--safe --skip-deferral` restart still waits on the restart-requesting turn's own pending tool/final replies because `skipDeferral` was not threaded through the restart intent to the server close logic.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/fix-skip-deferral-drain`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Build from branch and verify fix is in compiled production code

Step 2. Verify restart scheduler threads `skipDeferral` through the intent

Step 3. Verify server close returns 0 drain timeout when `skipDeferral` is true

Step 4. Run tests to confirm behavior

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the patched build:

The fix is active in the compiled restart scheduler (`dist/restart-DKRMfbtD.js`):

```console
$ grep -n 'scheduledSkipDeferral' dist/restart-DKRMfbtD.js
661:const scheduledSkipDeferral = pendingRestartSkipDeferral;
668:if (scheduledSkipDeferral || !pendingCheck) {
669:emitPreparedGatewayRestart(void 0, scheduledReason, scheduledSkipDeferral ? {
```

The compiled intent now carries `skipDeferral: true`:

```console
$ sed -n '668,674p' dist/restart-DKRMfbtD.js
if (scheduledSkipDeferral || !pendingCheck) {
emitPreparedGatewayRestart(void 0, scheduledReason, scheduledSkipDeferral ? {
skipDeferral: true,
...scheduledReason ? { reason: scheduledReason } : {}
} : void 0);
return;
}
```

The compiled run-loop (`dist/run-BlBIf3dQ.js`) returns 0 drain timeout for skip-deferral restarts:

```console
$ grep -B2 -A1 'skipDeferral' dist/run-BlBIf3dQ.js
const resolveRestartCloseDrainTimeoutMs = () => {
if (!isRestart) return null;
if (restartIntent?.skipDeferral) return 0;
if (restartDrainTimeoutMs === void 0) return Math.max(0, SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS - RESTART_CLOSE_REPLY_DRAIN_SHUTDOWN_RESERVE_MS);
```

Terminal confirms the behavioral change (9/9 tests pass, including 2 new tests for `skipDeferral` intent threading):

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts
 ✓ |infra| src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts (9 tests) 13ms
   ✓ deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle timeout > waits indefinitely when maxWaitMs is not specified
   ✓ deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle timeout > respects custom maxWaitMs configuration
   ✓ deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle timeout > clamps oversized poll intervals instead of polling immediately
   ✓ deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle timeout > carries timeout restart intent when the deferral budget is exhausted
   ✓ deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle timeout > calls onReady and does not timeout when pending count drops to 0
   ✓ deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle timeout > immediately restarts when pending count is 0
   ✓ deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle timeout > handles getPendingCount error by restarting immediately
   ✓ scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart skipDeferral intent > carries skipDeferral in the restart intent when skipDeferral is true
   ✓ scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart skipDeferral intent > does not carry skipDeferral when skipDeferral is false
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
```

Gateway starts and shuts down cleanly with patched code:

```console
$ timeout 8 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-06-02T13:12:07.744-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration...
2026-06-02T13:12:07.776-07:00 [gateway] resolving authentication...
2026-06-02T13:12:07.784-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-06-02T13:12:08.318-07:00 [gateway] starting HTTP server...
2026-06-02T13:12:08.346-07:00 [health-monitor] started
2026-06-02T13:12:15.204-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-06-02T13:12:15.205-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
```

Observed result after fix: Terminal output confirms `skipDeferral` is threaded from the restart scheduler through the intent to the close drain resolver. The compiled code at `dist/restart-DKRMfbtD.js:669` passes `{ skipDeferral: true }` in the intent, and `dist/run-BlBIf3dQ.js:385` returns `0` drain timeout when that flag is present. The new tests verify the intent carries `skipDeferral: true` after scheduling, and the gateway starts/stops cleanly.

What was not tested: Live `--safe --skip-deferral` restart with active pending replies from a channel-originated turn. The fix is verified at the intent-threading and timeout-resolution layers; end-to-end restart with live channel delivery requires configured channel credentials.
target.author: sebtardif
